Title: ZmWRKY92-Mediated miR169s/NF-YA13 Module Confers Maize Resistance to Bipolaris maydis by Activating Flavonoid Biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Its precursor and mature forms are repressed by ZmWRKY92 and induced in zmwrky92 mutants; induced previously during defense context
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in ZmWRKY92 overexpression lines during maize-pathogen interaction context
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial defense metabolites that contribute to resistance against Bipolaris maydis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of flavonoid-associated defense against Bipolaris maydis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. zma-miR169s is directly repressed by ZmWRKY92 through promoter binding. 2. It targets ZmNF-YA13 and negatively regulates maize defense. 3. Suppression of zma-miR169s allows activation of the flavonoid pathway and accumulation of antimicrobial flavonoids.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previous target validation cited; RT-qPCR of precursor and mature miRNA in ZmWRKY92 overexpression and mutant lines; Y1H; dual luciferase; EMSA
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous zma-miR169s promoter; 2-kb promoter used in LUC reporter assay
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for reducing susceptibility by editing or suppressing miR169s activity to elevate ZmNF-YA13 and flavonoid-mediated defense
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This study focused on its upstream transcriptional repression and downstream signaling role rather than revalidating its cleavage function.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY family TF; W-box-binding WRKY domain; C-terminal region required for immunity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly and sustainedly induced at 6, 12 and 24 h post inoculation with Bipolaris maydis, then declines by 36 hpi
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in infected maize leaves after Bipolaris maydis inoculation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial defense compounds contributing to resistance against Bipolaris maydis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes maize resistance to Bipolaris maydis by repressing zma-miR169s, thereby relieving suppression of ZmNF-YA13 and activating flavonoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmWRKY92 directly binds the zma-miR169s promoter W-box and represses its transcription. 2. Overexpression of ZmWRKY92 lowers pre-miR169s and mature zma-miR169s levels and enhances resistance to Bipolaris maydis. 3. Loss-of-function EMS mutants are more susceptible, showing increased miR169s expression. 4. ZmWRKY92 acts upstream of the miR169s/ZmNF-YA13/flavonoid defense module.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter prediction; RT-qPCR under infection; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; EMSA; maize overexpression; EMS mutant analysis; disease index; fungal biomass qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bipolaris maydis infection by spray inoculation with 10^5 spores/mL
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses transcription of zma-miR169s, indirectly increasing ZmNF-YA13 and downstream flavonoid biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (transient effector construct for assay); endogenous context in maize for function
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent upstream activator of flavonoid accumulation through repression of miR169s during pathogen response
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate upstream regulatory target for engineering disease-resistant maize by strengthening the WRKY92-miR169s-NF-YA13-flavonoid module; caution that C-terminal integrity is required for immunity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: An EMS mutant lacking 36 C-terminal amino acids still binds DNA in vitro but loses immune function, indicating DNA binding alone is insufficient for full activity.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots, leaves, tassels, ears, husks and silks; highest in tassels and ears; induced by Bipolaris maydis and peaks at 48 hpi
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in leaves and induced upon Bipolaris maydis infection
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial compounds that inhibit Bipolaris maydis growth and contribute to maize disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates flavonoid biosynthesis and promotes accumulation of antimicrobial flavonoids, thereby enhancing maize resistance to Bipolaris maydis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmNF-YA13 is a nuclear-localized target of zma-miR169s and is induced by Bipolaris maydis infection. 2. Overexpression enhances resistance, whereas CRISPR knockout increases susceptibility. 3. Integrated transcriptomics and metabolomics show activation of the flavonoid biosynthesis pathway in overexpression lines. 4. Overexpression elevates expression of key flavonoid biosynthetic genes and increases quercetin and gallocatechin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization in maize protoplasts; tissue expression profiling; infection time-course RT-qPCR; maize overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; western blot; disease index; fungal biomass qPCR; transcriptomics; metabolomics; RT-qPCR of pathway genes; HPLC metabolite quantification
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bipolaris maydis infection by spray inoculation with 10^5 spores/mL
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (RNA-seq, no accession provided); non-targeted metabolomics (no accession provided)
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation or positive regulation of pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Flavonoid biosynthesis pathway genes including HCT, CHI, F3'H and FLS
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates multiple flavonoid biosynthetic genes; most HCT, CHS, CHI, FLS and F3'H genes are induced in overexpression backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Relief from zma-miR169s-mediated repression; Bipolaris maydis infection; upstream ZmWRKY92 repression of zma-miR169s
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubiquitin promoter (overexpression construct); 35S promoter for GFP localization assay
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive downstream controller that links the WRKY92/miR169s regulatory cascade to flavonoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding or engineering maize resistance by boosting antimicrobial flavonoid production; can be combined with upstream WRKY92 or miR169s manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Integrated multi-omics identified flavonoid biosynthesis as the main enriched metabolic pathway associated with ZmNF-YA13 overexpression.
